title: 10 Buying Foreign Currency from a Customer
body_text:
10	Buying Foreign Currency from a Customer
10.1	Overview of Buying Foreign Currency from a Customer
Before processing a foreign currency purchase in FX Notes, you must verify the authenticity of the notes. Carefully compare the foreign currency notes to the corresponding image details in FX Notes. See Section 11.3, Viewing a List of Available Foreign Currencies and Their Images, to view images of foreign currency notes. If available, use a counterfeit detection light to verify the authenticity of the notes as well.
To buy foreign currency from a customer, a standard user creates a Buy from Client transaction on FX Notes, then ships the currency to Globex聽2000 the same day.
Note:	If suspected counterfeit foreign currency is sold to your financial institution, retain the notes and contact the RCMP or the local police department per your internal policy.
Important
:	If foreign currency is received from a customer and the amount is $10,000 CAD equivalent or more, a FINTRAC Large Cash Transaction Report must be submitted.
Important
:	If foreign currency is received from a customer and the amount is $3,000 CAD equivalent or more, ensure that the individual芒聙聶s name, address, and date of birth are on record. Your financial institution must also comply with requirements to ascertain identity and keep records for foreign currency exchange transactions.
Contact your AML Compliance Officer or FINTRAC for more information. Subscribers to the Operations Manual Program can also refer to the PCMLTFA Compliance Part.
10.2	Using Images to Authenticate Foreign Currency Notes
Visually assess the quality and details of the foreign currency in comparison to the images displayed in FX Notes. Authentic currency notes exhibit very detailed printing techniques. Visible and consistent printing and other physical attributes (e.g., holograms) should be identifiable.
For example, when comparing two 芒聜卢50 notes (one authentic and one counterfeit), the authentic note displays detailed cross hatching in the 芒聙聹50芒聙聺. The counterfeit note does not. See Figures 1 and 2.
Figure 1: Authentic Currency Printing
Figure 2: Counterfeit Currency Printing
Another example of detail verification is examining holograms. For example, when comparing the holograms on two 芒聜卢50 notes (one authentic and one counterfeit), the authentic note芒聙聶s hologram is very detailed (e.g., EURO on the perimeter of the hologram and the 芒聙聹芒聜卢芒聙聺 symbol behind the note芒聙聶s denomination are easily identifiable) while the counterfeit芒聙聶s hologram isn芒聙聶t detailed. See Figures 3 and 4.
Figure 3: Authentic Hologram
Figure 4: Counterfeit Hologram
Note:	Not all foreign currency notes have cross hatching or holograms, but for those that do, the details in printing should be apparent upon visual and physical examination of the note.
Procedures on accessing images when buying currency from a client are provided in the following section. Currency images are also available from the Denominations option on the left-hand menu. See Section 11.3, Viewing a List of Available Foreign Currencies and Their Images.
10.3	Buying Foreign Currency
A standard user proceeds as follows to buy foreign currency from a customer:
Log in to FX Notes as described in Section 6.2, Logging in to FX Notes.
Select
Buy from Client
on the home page. See Figure 5.
Figure 5: Select 芒聙聹Buy from Client芒聙聺
Complete the order form as follows. See Figures 6 and 7.
Figure 6: Currency Order Form
芒聭
Branch Location
芒聙聯 Select a branch from the Branch Location drop-down menu if you have access to multiple branches. If you only have access to one branch, it芒聙聶s displayed in this field by default.
芒聭隆
Pricing Options
芒聙聯 Select your foreign exchange spread from the Pricing Options drop-down menu. The options available for selection reflect any foreign exchange spreads you previously set up. See Chapter 7, Managing FX Notes Foreign Exchange Spreads and Transaction Fees.
芒聭垄
Product Type
芒聙聯 The product type is always CASH. This field isn芒聙聶t editable.
芒聭拢
Transaction Fee
芒聙聯 Select
Yes
or
No
to include your financial institution芒聙聶s transaction fee. The amount is displayed in the Transaction Fee field in the bottom right-hand corner of the page. Users can add, change, or remove the fee, per internal policy. Your transaction fees are managed on the Retail Rate Modifiers page. See Chapter 7, Managing FX Notes Foreign Exchange Spreads and Transaction Fees.
芒聭陇
Currency
芒聙聯 Select a currency from the drop-down menu.
芒聭楼
Amount
芒聙聯 Enter the exact amount of foreign currency that the customer wants to sell (e.g.,聽芒聜卢2,000). The Canadian dollar equivalent will show in the CAD Value field. Alternatively, enter the Canadian dollar amount as described in step 8 below.
芒聭娄
Rate
芒聙聯 The Globex 2000 exchange rate is displayed. This field isn芒聙聶t editable.
Important:
Rates are held for 15 minutes and can be refreshed at any time.
芒聭搂
CAD Value
芒聙聯 The foreign currency equivalent is displayed in the Amount field.
芒聭篓
Verify Currency
芒聙聯 Select
View Currency
to view images of the foreign currency notes.
芒聭漏
Select an image of the applicable denomination. Images of both the front and back of the note are provided. See Figure 7.
Figure 7: Select Image of Applicable Denomination
芒聭陋
View the enlarged image and compare it to the physical currency note. See Figure 8. Additional information on assessing the images is provided in Section 10.2, Using Images to Authenticate Foreign Currency Notes.
Important:
If the images on FX Notes don芒聙聶t match the foreign currency, don芒聙聶t buy it. If you suspect that counterfeit foreign currency is presented, retain the notes and contact the RCMP or the local police department per your internal policy
.
Figure 8: Detailed Image
Denomination Breakdown
芒聙聯 Select specific denominations in the Denomination Breakdown section. See Figure 9.
Figure 9: Denomination Breakdown
Add Currency
芒聙聯 Select
Add Currency
if the customer wants to sell multiple foreign currencies. Repeat the steps described above.
Special Requests / Printable Notes
芒聙聯 Enter any notes or special requests.
Select
Submit Order
.
Select
Confirm
to proceed with the order. See Figure 10.
Figure 10: Confirm Order
View the currency order receipt, then print a copy for the customer using the browser芒聙聶s print function. The receipt displays the total order amount including the Canadian dollar equivalent, your transaction fee, and the shipping fee. All fees are deducted from the amount. Your financial institution芒聙聶s logo is displayed in the top right-hand corner. See Figure 11.
Figure 11: Currency Order Receipt
Credit your customer for the amount indicated in the 芒聙聹Total Due from Client芒聙聺 field. See Figure 11.
Prepare the foreign currency for shipment as described in the following section.
AML Considerations
Contact your financial institution芒聙聶s AML Compliance Officer or FINTRAC for more information on the requirements for foreign exchange transactions. This may include completing a foreign exchange transaction ticket. Subscribers to the Operations Manual Program can use the Foreign Exchange Transaction Ticket, Form 1923, and refer to the PCMLTFA Compliance Part for more information.
Related Information
Foreign Exchange Transaction Ticket,
Form 1923
(available to Operations Manual Program subscribers only)
10.4	Shipping Foreign Currency to Globex 2000
UPS is the main courier used to ship currency to Globex 2000. However, for remote areas that UPS doesn芒聙聶t service, use Canada Post or drop off the currency at the nearest UPS access point. Globex聽2000 determines the shipping method when a financial institution is set up with FX Notes. If Canada Post is necessary, instructions will be provided during enrollment.
When preparing foreign currency for shipment, only use opaque security bags and the UPS envelope for packaging. Each package can contain up to $5,000 CAD equivalent and a maximum of two packages can be sent per order, $10,000 per order.
Important:
Ensure that all packages are packed in a well-lit space, under cameras if feasible, and in dual custody. Video footage helps support any claims made.
If a single order shipment of foreign currency is $10,000 or more (CAD equivalent), contact Globex聽2000 for additional instructions on shipping multiple packages. See Section 1.3 Contacts, Websites, and Other Resources.
Foreign currency shipments must contain a copy of the Buying Operation and Client Receipt. Ensure that shipments are prepared in dual custody in front of a camera.
Shipping supplies, such as security bags and courier envelopes, are automatically replenished based on your usage levels. However, if your shipping supplies are running low, contact
nmailloux@ups.com
to request more.
To ship foreign currency:
Log in to FX Notes as described in Section 6.2, Logging in to FX Notes.
Select
Transactional
, then
Shipments
on the left-hand menu. See Figure 12.
Figure 12: Navigate to Shipments
Select the ellipsis
(...) icon beside the shipment you芒聙聶re interested in, then select
Download Label
or
Download Packing Slip
. See Figure 13.
Figure 13: Select 芒聙聹Download Label芒聙聺 or 芒聙聹Download Packing Slip芒聙聺
After the documents have downloaded, print out each document using your browser芒聙聶s print function. See Figure 14 for examples of the shipping label and packing slip.
Figure 14: Example Shipping Label and Packing Slip
Important:
Shipping labels and packing slips can only be printed if the foreign currency order has a 芒聙聹Pending Reception芒聙聺 status. To confirm the order芒聙聶s status, see Section 11.1, Viewing Today芒聙聶s Purchases and Sales, for more information.
Group the foreign currency notes by currency, then by denomination (e.g., 芒聜卢5, 芒聜卢10, 芒聜卢20). Bundle the currency from highest to lowest denominations and face them in the same direction.
Place the currency bundle, the applicable receipt, and packing slip in the opaque security bag supplied by Globex 2000. Seal the opaque security bag. See Figure 15.
Figure 15: Packaging Foreign Currency
Place the sealed opaque security bag in the courier envelope. See Figure 16.
Note:	You can
insert multiple opaque security bags in the courier envelope if the currency total doesn芒聙聶t exceed $5,000 (CAD equivalent).
Figure 16: Placing Opaque Bag into Courier Envelope
Seal the envelope and ensure the package is well secured with tape on either end.
Use a permanent marker to leave a noticeable signature along the sealed portion of the taped envelope. See Figure 17.
Figure 17: Signing the Sealed Envelope
Place the shipping label inside the courier label pouch.
Seal the courier envelope and adhere the label pouch onto the envelope. See Figure 18.
Figure 18: Shipping Label Placement
Call UPS to order a courier pick up at 1-800-742-5877. Globex 2000 recommends that you call, rather than order your pick-up online, to ensure you get personal confirmation of the order.
Tell the representative that pick-up is required for an overnight delivery.
If asked for your financial institution芒聙聶s account number or payment information, say that you have a prepaid label and everything is in the receiver芒聙聶s name (i.e., Globex聽2000).
The courier company requires the
financial institution芒聙聶s contact person芒聙聶s name,
financial institution芒聙聶s address and phone number,
financial institution芒聙聶s hours of operation, and
weight of the package, which is always less than 1 lb.
If asked about the size of the package, say that it is a UPS envelope.
Caution
:	If asked about the contents of the shipment, say 芒聙聹documents芒聙聺. In no circumstance should you indicate that currency is the content of the shipment.
Important
:	You must request pick-up for currency shipments on business days only. Don芒聙聶t request a courier pick-up on Saturday. Foreign currency shipments must be picked up by the courier company芒聙聶s driver. Don芒聙聶t use a drop box. See Figure 19.
Figure 19: Courier Pickup
Ensure the courier company芒聙聶s employee scans each package they pick up.
Globex 2000 won芒聙聶t confirm when packages are received, but if a package is
not
received, Globex聽2000 will contact your financial institution.
END
